repository_ssim: St. Catherine's Monastery of the Sinai
titles_tesim: Troparia for Saint Macrina the Younger
contents_tesim: The folios contain troparia (one kathisma and four stichera) to be performed on the feast of Saint Macrina the Younger, sister of Saint Basil the Great and Gregory of Nyssa, on 19th July (see MR VI 159–168) (Rossetto)
Troparia for Saint Macrina the Younger
full_text_tesim: One bifolio of the present manuscript is constituted of one original folio, measured approximately 200x150 mm. The Schriftspiegel is approximately 155x90 mm, with large margins: the upper measures 35 mm (f. 185v), the outer 55 mm (f. 185v), and the lower 40 mm (f. 184r)
Prickings are still visible in the outer margin. They are elongated rather than round, probably made with a penknife. The ruled lines correspond to Sautel-Leroy type 00A1 (Rossetto)
Small and elegant, upright early minuscule. Simple initials introduce each troparion and are located outside the text block. Titles are written in Alexandrian majuscule

The folios contain troparia (one kathisma and four stichera) to be performed on the feast of Saint Macrina the Younger, sister of Saint Basil the Great and Gregory of Nyssa, on 19th July (see MR VI 159–168) (Rossetto)
Troparia for Saint Macrina the Younger
